input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 10 (which targets Android API 35+), edge-to-edge display is enforced, and the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach was deprecated. The new approach uses `WindowInsets` and the `ImeInsets` to handle keyboard appearance.


In .NET 10, `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` is no longer effective because edge-to-edge mode is the default on Android 15+. Instead, I need to handle keyboard insets properly through the `WindowInsets` API to adjust the layout when the IME appears.

The most practical approach in MAUI is to either use `adjustPan` in the AndroidManifest to pan the view up, or bind padding to keyboard inset changes through platform-specific handlers. The better approach for chat interfaces is to listen for window insets directly on Android, detecting when the keyboard appears and adjusting the bottom padding of the layout to accommodate it.
